lessons.title: EU Citizenship — Origins, Nature and the Maastricht Innovation
lessons.description: Understanding EU citizenship is essential for FE-1 candidates because it underpins the rights of free movement, equal treatment, and political participation that arise throughout EU law.

lessons.title: Right of Residence Under Directive 2004/38 — Conditions and Welfare
lessons.description: The Directive consolidates and replaces a series of earlier instruments and creates a tiered residence framework that rewards longer residence with more secure status.

lessons.title: Third Country National Family Members — Zambrano, McCarthy and Dereci
lessons.description: This area is defined by three landmark cases: Ruiz Zambrano , McCarthy , and Dereci . Understanding these cases and the limits they impose on each other is essential for FE-1 candidates.

lessons.title: Political Rights, Consular Protection and Constitutional Significance
lessons.description: the final lesson of Module Eleven. In our previous lessons we have examined the right of residence under Directive 2004/38 and the Zambrano-McCarthy-Dereci jurisprudence on third country national family members.
